obligations.govai_mapping: Continuous risk treatment is supported by policy-backed evidence and exportable audit trails; operators document residual risk outside GovAI.
obligations.summary: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system applied throughout the entire lifecycle.
obligations.title: Risk management system

obligations.govai_mapping: Dataset and model lineage can be referenced in governance evidence packs; GovAI does not certify data quality legal sufficiency.
obligations.summary: Training, validation, and testing data shall be subject to appropriate governance practices relevant to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.
obligations.title: Data and data governance

obligations.govai_mapping: Technical documentation packs can cite GovAI exports as append-only evidence of controls exercised during development and deployment preparation.
obligations.summary: High-risk AI systems shall be accompanied by technical documentation demonstrating compliance with the requirements set out in Title III, Chapter 2.
obligations.title: Technical documentation

obligations.govai_mapping: Transparency obligations for deployers intersect with logging and disclosure artefacts; mapping doc explains indicative alignment only.
obligations.summary: Automatic recording of events (logs) shall be enabled by design over the lifetime of the high-risk AI system to the extent appropriate.
obligations.title: Record-keeping

obligations.govai_mapping: Human approval gates in documentation mirror procedural oversight; runtime kill-switch and escalation remain operator-owned.
obligations.summary: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed so that natural persons can effectively oversee their functioning during the period in which the AI system is in use.
obligations.title: Human oversight

obligations.govai_mapping: Accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity are addressed in enterprise security docs; this file links high-risk themes to GovAI non-claims.
obligations.summary: High-risk AI systems shall achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity in light of their intended purpose.
obligations.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity

obligations.govai_mapping: Conformity procedures remain with qualified assessors; GovAI supplies reproducible technical exports to attach to dossiers when counsel approves.
obligations.summary: High-risk AI systems shall undergo the relevant conformity assessment procedure prior to being placed on the market or put into service.
obligations.title: Conformity assessment

obligations.govai_mapping: Post-market evidence can include recurring compliance summaries and incident exports where operators wire monitoring to GovAI-backed audit services.
obligations.summary: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies.
obligations.title: Post-market monitoring by providers

obligations.govai_mapping: Annex IV structure informs the export guide; generated Markdown is a convenience bundle, not a completed Annex IV submission.
obligations.summary: Technical documentation shall contain the elements set out in Annex IV, where applicable to the AI system in question.
obligations.title: Annex IV technical documentation elements

obligations.govai_mapping: Deployer obligations for monitoring and human oversight intersect with GovAI run lifecycle; matrix maps themes to repository artefacts.
obligations.summary: Deployers shall monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system in accordance with the instructions for use and perform human oversight as required.
obligations.title: Deployer monitoring and oversight duties

summary: Structured index of indicative EU AI Act obligations referenced by GovAI regulatory documentation. Articles are cited for operator orientation; definitive legal obligations follow the Official Journal text and implementing acts.
